fields.content: “So be strong and courageous! Do not be afraid and do not panic before them. For the Lord your God will personally go ahead of you. He will neither fail you nor abandon you.”

fields.title: Word Of Truth
fields.summary: “But he answered and said, It is written, Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that proceedeth out of the mouth of God.”
fields.body:
Matthew 4:4 (KJV)

“But he answered and said, It is written, Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that proceedeth out of the mouth of God.”

This is Jesus responding to Satan during the temptation in the wilderness.


The full temptation passage (Matthew 4:1–11)

A different verse where Jesus says “It is written”

A short teaching explanation to go with it for a slide or message.

fields.title: Q and A with BBI School of Ministry Director
fields.summary: Discover how BBI School of Ministry equips believers in deliverance and spiritual warfare through Holy Spirit-led training with global reach
fields.body:
Q: Please introduce yourself and tell us more about the BBI School of Ministry course load and how you have performed.

A: My name is Irene Mbithe, and I serve as the Director of Training at BBI School of Ministry. I oversee our trainers as we develop, test, and roll out deliverance and spiritual warfare training programs.

Everything we do is led and accelerated by the Holy Spirit. In fact, through His guidance, we developed our Level 1 Deliverance and Spiritual Warfare Training in just three weeks—a remarkable milestone.

Since September 2023, we have trained 29 students in Level 1. This foundational course establishes a strong biblical understanding of deliverance. We are now transitioning our training fully online to increase accessibility and global reach.

We are currently developing Level 2 and Level 3 programs, designed to take students deeper and equip them to operate confidently as deliverance ministers within their spheres of influence.

 

Q: What do you want to achieve with the next-level cohort at BBI School of Ministry?

A: Our vision at BBI is to see every believer in the Lord Jesus Christ set free from invisible barriers that prevent them from fulfilling their God-given purpose.

Our mission is to equip believers to live victoriously and fulfill their calling through the inspiration of the Holy Spirit.

Before enrolling in training, our students go through deliverance to break spiritual hindrances. The transformation we witness is powerful. As students understand their identity and authority in Christ, they adopt a “David versus Goliath” mindset. The freedom and confidence that follow are truly phenomenal.

 

Q: What are the main challenges facing deliverance ministry training schools, and how do you plan to address them?

A: One major challenge is that many ministries shy away from deliverance. Yet deliverance is essential for every believer. In the early church, new believers underwent deliverance before baptism—this practice has largely been lost.

Another challenge is unconscious incompetence—many believers don’t know deliverance exists, even as they struggle with invisible barriers.

Fear also plays a role. Some think ignoring the devil makes him go away. But Scripture is clear: we have an enemy. The good news is that Jesus defeated Satan at the cross.

We address these challenges through biblical teaching, equipping believers with truth, and serving as a kingdom resource ready to collaborate and learn alongside others.

 

Q: What sets BBI School of Ministry apart in deliverance training?

A: BBI School of Ministry is built upon the teachings of our founder and CEO, Pastor Nellie Shani. She has a strong biblical foundation and was mentored by The Navigators.

Our training is Holy Spirit-inspired and Bible-based—not rooted in human opinion. Scripture is our final authority.

 

Q: What are your top priorities at the school?

A: Our highest priority is alignment with the Holy Spirit. We remain anchored to our vision and mission and move only in the direction He leads.

 

Q: Tell us about a difficult situation you recently overcame concerning course delivery.

A: We initially planned to launch Level 1 Cohort 3 in February 2024. However, due to registration numbers, we postponed to March to allow more time for enrollment.

We are also trusting God for more trainers. The harvest is plentiful, but the laborers are few.

 

Q: What are your long-term goals?

A: Our long-term vision is global accessibility. We want believers anywhere in the world—Antarctica, Pakistan, Alaska, Japan, Mauritania—to access our online deliverance training and experience transformation.

 

Q: What would you like to see done differently in deliverance training generally?

A: The myth that deliverance is only for a chosen few must end. Jesus instructed His disciples to heal the sick, raise the dead, cleanse lepers, and cast out demons (Matthew 10:8).

Deliverance is part of the Great Commission. Believers must walk confidently in their identity and authority in Christ.

 

Q: How has your previous experience prepared you for this role?

Pst Dr Nellie Shani, BBI Founder and CEO

A: When I reflect on my journey, I see God’s preparation. I gave my life to Christ about 10 years ago. Salvation transformed me. As I studied Scripture, I realized we had limited God in many churches. I longed to see the God who parted the Red Sea at work today.

In 2015, I experienced deliverance through a BBI member and began formal study with various ministries. I hold a practitioner certification from Global Awakening College of Ministry in physical healing, emotional healing, and deliverance. I am pursuing Master Equippers certification and am enrolled in the Global Supernatural Discipleship (GSD) course.

Before ministry, I spent over 19 years in corporate leadership and trained as an aerospace engineer.

 

Q: How will you measure success at BBI School of Ministry?

A: Success means seeing believers understand their identity in Christ, live intimately with God, and walk victoriously in their purpose—doing great exploits for the Kingdom.

 

Q: What is the latest skill you learned, and why?

A: Emotional healing. I trained with Connect Up because emotional wounds are widespread. Inner healing and deliverance work hand in hand. We must learn to bring our pain to Jesus.

 

Q: Is there anything else you would like to share?

A: I want to honor Pastor Nellie Shani for her obedience to God’s call and for creating a platform where believers can grow and fellowship.

I honor the BBI faculty and trainers for their dedication, and our courageous students who desire to go deeper in God. Above all, I thank God for the privilege of serving in His Kingdom.

 

About BBI School of Ministry

BBI School of Ministry is a Holy Spirit-led training ground committed to equipping believers in deliverance, spiritual warfare, emotional healing, and biblical identity—preparing them to impact their communities and nations for Christ.

Click on this link https://forms.gle/G6P6QDGDiPvSPzfo6 to register in our School of Ministry.

fields.title: BBI Updates for Members
fields.summary: BBI updates its ministry policies to ensure a safe, Spirit-filled community. Learn about new Zoom prayer watch rules, financial safety, and online deliverance guidelines.
fields.body:
Strengthening Our Community: Updates & Safety Guidelines

BELOVED BBI family, we are grateful for God’s sustaining grace in our journey of faith, prayer, and transformation. Together, we cultivate a safe, loving, and accountable environment where every member is encouraged, supported, and uplifted in Christ.

 

Addressing Recent Security Concerns

Recently, our ministry platform experienced an unfortunate incident where a stranger infiltrated our space and fraudulently extorted money from a few members. While this grieves us deeply, we are responding in wisdom, not fear, implementing guidelines to strengthen our spiritual and digital walls and protect our sacred community.

 

“Like Nehemiah rebuilding Jerusalem’s walls, we are building spiritual walls of discernment, integrity, and love.”

 

New Financial Policies

To protect members and ensure transparency:

  • No one should request money during Zoom sessions.

  • If approached with personal financial requests, notify administrator Pauline immediately.

  • All legitimate needs will be processed through the BBI administration in consultation with CEO Pastor Nellie Shani.

“Our community thrives when clarity, honesty, and compassion guide every interaction.” – Pastor Nellie Shani

Zoom Meeting Guidelines

To maintain a secure and focused environment:

  1. Prayer Watch Access – Participation requires attendance at an online deliverance session. Register through the administrator before joining.

  2. Digital Security – Do not forward Zoom links, calendars, or ministry materials to outsiders.

  3. Name Identification – Use a full and recognizable name; avoid nicknames or device names (e.g., iPhone, Sam G).

  4. Corporate Prayer Culture – Actively participate in prayers; if unable to speak aloud, respond in chat.

  5. Participating in Breakthroughs – Occasionally, you may be asked to pray over your own request as others agree in prayer—this builds spiritual ownership.

Ministry Ethos: A Culture of Prayer and Growth

BBI is more than a gathering—we are a community of intercessors, deliverance ministers, prayer warriors, and teachers. Our values include:

  • Unity in the Spirit

  • Respect and honesty in communication

  • Spiritual maturity

  • Growth in prayer life

 

“We are called to be watchmen—not just for ourselves but for our brothers and sisters. Let us remain alert, prayerful, and supportive.” – Pastor Nellie

Moving Forward Together

BBI desires to maintain a safe, Spirit-filled ministry platform where members can grow, be healed, and experience divine encounters. By following these guidelines, we protect what God has entrusted to us and ensure that our community remains a haven for encouragement, accountability, and spiritual transformation.

Thank you for standing guard with us as we continue pressing forward for the glory of Jesus Christ.

fields.title: Rev Dan Ole Shani Urges Spiritual Vigilance
fields.summary: Rev Dan Ole Shani, Chairman of Breaking Barriers International, delivered a powerful message at Bread of Life Church Nairobi urging believers to endure to the end. Preaching from Matthew 24 and 2 Peter 1, he warned about love growing cold in the last days and called Christians to perseverance, repentance, and spiritual alertness.
fields.body:
REV Dan Ole Shani, Chairman of Breaking Barriers International (BBI), delivered a timely and sobering message at Bread of Life Church,Nairobi,Kenya urging believers to “endure to the end in our Christian life.”

 

Preaching from Matthew 24:12–14, Rev. Shani reminded the congregation that true discipleship is not merely about beginning the journey of faith with passion, but finishing it with perseverance.

 

“Because lawlessness will abound, the love of many will grow cold. But he who endures to the end shall be saved.” — Jesus Christ

 

He emphasized that perseverance is not optional for Christians — it is the very evidence of authentic faith.

 

Love Growing Cold in the Last Days

Drawing from 2 Timothy 3:1–5, Rev. Shani outlined 19 characteristics that signal spiritual decline in the last days. The apostle Paul warned that people would become:

 

  • Lovers of self

  • Lovers of money

  • Proud and arrogant

  • Abusive and disobedient

  • Ungrateful and unholy

  • Heartless and unforgiving

  • Slanderous and without self-control

  • Brutal and not lovers of good

  • Treacherous and reckless

  • Swollen with conceit

  • Lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God

  • Having a form of godliness but denying its power

 

Rev. Shani cautioned that when a believer begins to exhibit these traits, it signals a dangerous drift away from intimacy with Christ.

 

“Like the proverbial frog placed in gradually heated water, many believers may not notice the spiritual decline until it is too late,” he warned.

 

He explained that spiritual deception deepens when warning signs are ignored, godly counsel is resisted, and the conviction of the Holy Spirit is continually suppressed.

 

Bread of Life Church, which serves Nairobi’s Chinese Christian community, received the message with sober reflection as Rev. Shani urged them to remain spiritually alert in what he described as a VUCA (volatile, uncertain, complex, ambiguous) world.

 

 

How to Persevere Until the End

Turning to 2 Peter 1:5–7, Rev. Shani outlined eight qualities every believer must intentionally cultivate to safeguard their faith:

 

  1. Faith – Trusting in God’s promises

  2. Virtue (Goodness) – Choosing moral excellence

  3. Knowledge – Growing in understanding of God’s Word

  4. Self-control – Mastering desires and impulses

  5. Perseverance – Standing firm under trial

  6. Godliness – Living reverently before God

  7. Brotherly kindness – Loving fellow believers

  8. Love – The crowning virtue that reflects Christ

 

“These qualities do not develop automatically,” he said. “Peter urges us to make every effort. Perseverance requires intentional pursuit, daily surrender, spiritual discipline, and reliance on the Holy Spirit.”

 

Referring to 2 Peter 1:10–11, he challenged believers to diligently confirm their calling and election.

“Enduring to the end is about clinging to Christ, abiding in His Word, and depending fully on the Spirit of God.”

 

 

A Call to Repentance and Spiritual Alertness

Rev. Shani’s message was both a warning and an invitation. He called the church to repentance, vigilance, and renewed devotion to Christ.

 

In a world marked by increasing lawlessness and spiritual compromise, he urged believers not to drift with cultural currents but to press forward with endurance fueled by love for Jesus.

 

The message concluded with Christ’s enduring promise:

“He who endures to the end shall be saved.

fields.title: Year of Miracles, Signs and Wonders | BBI 2026 Message
fields.summary: BBI’s 2026 prophetic theme: Manifesting the Kingdom Through the Word.  A year of walking in bold faith, obedience and God’s Word to release miracles, fruitfulness, and Kingdom manifestation in your life.
fields.body:
SHALOM Saints. Praise God.

 

“Now, Lord, look on their threats, and grant to Your servants that with all boldness they may speak Your word, by stretching out Your hand to heal, and that signs and wonders may be done through the name of Your holy Servant Jesus.” — Acts of the Apostles 4:29–30

 

2026: A Year of Spiritual Explosion

This is the year of:

  • A spiritual explosion

  • Miracles, signs, and wonders

  • Bold proclamation of the Word of God

  • The visible manifestation of God’s Kingdom

 

In 2026, God will reveal Himself to:

  • Those who believe

  • Those who draw closer to Him

  • Those who listen and obey His voice

“Your ears shall hear a word behind you, saying, ‘This is the way, walk in it.’” — Book of Isaiah 30:21

“For the earnest expectation of the creation eagerly waits for the revealing of the sons of God.” — Epistle to the Romans 8:19

 

Creation is waiting for the manifestation of the sons of God.

 

What Does It Mean to Manifest the Kingdom?

To manifest means:

  • To show forth

  • To make visible

  • To bring into reality

 

The manifestation of God’s Kingdom happens through believers.

 

There must be no contradiction between:

  • What the Word of God says

  • What we display in our daily lives

 

God is not a man that He should lie (Book of Numbers 23:19).
What He promises, He performs (Book of Jeremiah 32:27).

 

Sons of God: Led by the Spirit

“For as many as are led by the Spirit of God, these are sons of God.” — Romans 8:14

“It is the Spirit who gives life; the flesh profits nothing. The words that I speak to you are spirit, and they are life.” — Gospel of John 6:63

 

To be led by the Spirit is to:

  • Believe the Word

  • Act upon the Word

  • Speak the Word in every situation

 

The Lord taught us to pray:

“Let Your Kingdom come, let Your will be done on earth as it is in Heaven.” — Gospel of Matthew 6:10

 

God’s will is His Word. When we pray the Word, we pray His will.

 

The Kingdom of God Is Within You

“The Kingdom of God is within you.” — Luke 17:21

 

The power to manifest is already within you.

  • The Spirit works in you (Epistle to the Philippians 2:13)

  • The same Spirit that raised Christ lives in you (Romans 8:11)

  • You have an anointing (First Epistle of John 2:20)

  • Greater is He who is in you (1 John 4:4)

  • You have authority (Gospel of Luke 10:19)

 

How Do We Manifest the Kingdom in 2026?

1. Through Our Mindset

“For as he thinks in his heart, so is he.” — Book of Proverbs 23:7

Kingdom manifestation begins in the heart and mind.
Transformation precedes manifestation.

 

2. By Speaking the Word with Boldness

Faith speaks.
Boldness releases Heaven’s power.

“Have faith in God…” — Gospel of Mark 11:22–24

The Word must precede the manifestation (Mark 16:20).

 

3. By Producing Fruit

The manifestation of the Kingdom is fruit.

God is looking for fruit in the lives of His children (Luke 13:6–9).
Miracles testify that the Father is at work (John 10:38).
Signs confirm that the Kingdom is active (Luke 7:22–23).

 

The Truth of 2026

  • The Kingdom of God is within you.

  • The Spirit of God is leading you.

  • The Word of God is alive in you.

  • The power to manifest is already inside you.

 

This is the year to:

  • Draw closer to God

  • Listen carefully to His voice

  • Speak His Word boldly

  • Walk in obedience

  • Expect miracles

 

Creation is waiting for your revealing.

Blessed be the Name of the Lord.

Pst Dr Nellie Shani

fields.title: Inside BBI New Digital Season
fields.summary: BBI ministry’s full transition to online platforms has expanded global reach through Zoom teachings, counseling, and group deliverance clinics since 2024.
fields.body:
THE Bible reminds us in Ecclesiastes 3:1:

“To everything there is a season, and a time to every purpose under heaven.”

 

For BBI, that season arrived when Founder and CEO Nellie Shani announced a major shift in the ministry’s direction during a worship night held on March 22, 2024.

 

After years of operating from Apex Court offices, BBI is now transitioning fully into an online ministry model.

 

“BBI is standing at the door of a new season as we grow and expand,” Pastor Nellie shared with members.

 

From Physical Office to Global Online Reach

 

For seven years, BBI operated from its Apex Court location, serving believers through in-person teachings, counseling, and deliverance sessions.

 

However, as Pastor Nellie explained:

“The need for our ministry in the body of Christ has grown exponentially.”

 

To meet this growing demand, BBI is fully migrating online.

 

Beginning at the end of April 2024:

  • All teachings will be conducted via Zoom

  • Counseling sessions will be virtual

  • Deliverance sessions will be hosted online

  • Morning prayer meetings will continue uninterrupted

 

This shift allows BBI to expand beyond geographical limitations and reach believers globally.

 

Diaspora Group Deliverance Clinics Growing Rapidly

 

The transition has already begun.

 

BBI recently launched diaspora group deliverance clinics, with 67 participants attending a recent session.

 

“It was an encouragement that God is moving us in the right direction,” Pastor Nellie said.

 

The online format enables greater participation from believers across Africa, the United States, the United Kingdom, Asia, and Eastern Europe—regions where Pastor Nellie has ministered over the years.

A Ministry Built on Deliverance and Spiritual Warfare

 

Founded 15 years ago, BBI is a non-profit Christian organization focused on:

  • Spiritual warfare training

  • Deliverance ministry

  • Equipping believers to live victoriously

  • Helping Christians fulfill their God-given purpose

 

BBI provides structured group sessions and support groups for men and women, meeting twice monthly after completing deliverance training.

 

Its core mission: to see the children of God set free.

Pastor Nellie Shani: Author, Speaker, Global Minister

 

Nellie Shani is an international conference speaker and workshop leader who has ministered across multiple continents.

 

She has lived in:

  • Zambia

  • Mauritania

  • Senegal

  • The United States

  • The United Kingdom

 

Over two decades, she says the Holy Spirit trained her in spiritual warfare and deliverance while serving abroad.

 

She has authored twelve books in her Spiritual Warfare series, including:

  • Breaking Invisible Barrier – The Power of the Cross

  • Breaking Financial Curses

  • Can Witchcraft Affect A Christian

  • Step into the Supernatural

  • Live the Abundant Life

 

In a previous interview, Pastor Nellie shared that she was inspired to found BBI after recognizing “patterns of failure not only in my family line but also in the family lines of Christians around me.”

Gratitude to Long-Standing Members

 

During the announcement, Pastor Nellie expressed gratitude to those who have faithfully supported BBI through the years.

 

“We have had many committed servants of God who have stood with me and we have co-laboured as we carried the Lord’s burden together.”

 

She especially acknowledged members who relinquished official positions yet remained actively connected and supportive of the ministry.

 

Many members testified that they first learned about BBI through referrals from friends and relatives—demonstrating the ministry’s growing grassroots impact.

Why the Shift to Online Ministry 

 

The move online represents:

  • Greater global reach

  • Increased accessibility

  • Expanded group deliverance capacity

  • Continued teaching and discipleship

  • Flexibility for diaspora communities

 

In today’s digital age, online ministry allows BBI to serve far beyond the limits of physical location.

 

As Pastor Nellie emphasized, this is not a reduction—it is an expansion.

Prayer for the New Season

 

Pastor Nellie called on members to pray:

“Please pray with us as we go through the logistics of managing this new season and as we grow and expand.”

 

The transition marks not an ending, but the beginning of a broader global impact.

 

There is indeed a season for everything. For BBI, the new season is digital, global, and expanding.

 

With deliverance clinics already drawing strong participation and Zoom platforms ready to host teachings, BBI is positioned to reach more believers than ever before.

 

The mission remains the same:
Equipping believers in Jesus Christ to walk in freedom and fulfill their God-given destiny.

fields.title: BBI Launches Solomon Project
fields.summary: Join BBI Solomon Project in Nairobi. Support a lasting centre for worship, discipleship, deliverance, and spiritual transformation.
fields.body:
Breaking Barriers International (BBI) proudly announces the launch of the BBI Solomon Project, a faith-driven initiative to acquire a permanent ministry building in Nairobi, Kenya.

 

This transformative project marks a historic milestone in BBI’s mission to strengthen its foundation, expand its impact, and establish a lasting legacy for generations of believers.

 

For over 15 years, BBI has been a trusted pillar of spiritual transformation, deliverance, and discipleship, touching lives locally and globally. Even after transitioning fully online in 2024, the ministry’s growth continues to highlight the urgent need for a stable physical centre to host its expanding activities.

 

The envisioned facility will serve as a hub for worship services, discipleship training, group deliverance sessions, community outreach programs, and ministry administration. Beyond bricks and mortar, this centre will be a beacon of faith, hope, and spiritual transformation, empowering individuals and communities alike.

 

BBI warmly invites partners, builders, and kingdom collaborators to join this generational vision. Registration is now open for all who desire to contribute, support, and participate in establishing a permanent altar for God’s work in Nairobi and beyond. Together, we can secure a lasting spiritual legacy that will impact lives for decades to come.

fields.title: Let's All Be Students of Deliverance in 2026
fields.summary: BBI is calling believers worldwide to become intentional students of deliverance and spiritual warfare in 2026, grounding themselves in Scripture and practical training to stand strong in spiritual battles.
fields.body:
DRAWING from Psalms 18: 31-43, believers are being reminded that God Himself trains our hands for battle and strengthens our feet for war. Scripture declares that He makes our feet like the feet of a deer and enables us to stand on the heights.

 

Just as mountain deer are sure-footed and stable on dangerous terrain, God equips His people with spiritual discernment and knowledge. He arms our minds, sharpens our understanding, and encourages us to advance confidently into spiritual warfare. We are warriors. We are battle axes in the hand of the Lord.

 

Let us be students of deliverance by reading BBI materials, watching and listening to BBI teachings on YouTube and Facebook, and attending BBI monthly teaching gatherings and School of Ministry.

 

“We are proud of the quality of our trainees who are committed and putting into practice what they have learnt. Most of them are taking up leadership roles in their churches and communities including digital spaces,” said Irene Mbithe, Director of BBI School of Ministry.

 

She noted that the last quarter drew trainees from the United States, India, Dubai, Zambia, and Malawi, with nearly all completing the program.

 

The call is clear- let us be students of spiritual warfare

 

To enroll, partner or learn more - email:
breakingbarrierzinternational@gmail.com

fields.title: BBI Monthly Teaching : Walking in Divine Health
fields.summary: BBI hosted Pastor Dr. Nellie Shani for a powerful teaching on healing as a finished work. Discover how to shift from asking to receiving.
fields.body:
NAIROBI, Kenya — On February 28, 2026, Breaking Barriers International (BBI) hosted its monthly teaching meeting, featuring a transformative session by Pastor Dr. Nellie Shani. The online gathering, titled "Walking in Divine Health," drew over 110 participants eager to understand the spiritual mechanics of healing.

Dr. Shani challenged traditional views on prayer and sickness, emphasizing that healing is a finished work of Christ. "God is not going to do what He already did," she noted, pointing to the sacrifice on the cross as the ultimate source of health.

The session focused on shifting from "begging" God for healing to "receiving" it by faith, using the biblical example of the withered fig tree to illustrate the power of spoken words and unwavering belief.

Click on this link for a related post https://bb-international.org/childrens-bread/divine-health-5-spiritual-barriers-and-breakthroughs/

The teaching resonated deeply with the global audience, as Dr. Shani dismantled common misconceptions—such as the idea that God uses sickness to teach lessons—and provided a roadmap for overcoming spiritual roadblocks to physical restoration.

fields.description:
A sacred time of early-day alignment with God. As the sun rises, we gather to seek the Lord’s face, present our families, destinies, and nations before Him, and receive strength for the day ahead. Rooted in Scripture and led by the Holy Spirit. It is a time to hear God clearly, enforce victory through prayer, and activate divine purpose. 

Prayers are led by BBI Intercessors every Monday, Thursday & Saturday.

TIME: 5.00am-6.00am EAT
